system.description:
See power: @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Boost/Lower Trait]{Boost/Lower Trait}

A wand is a thin baton that casts a single @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power}, including any @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{Power Modifiers}, each time it's activated. To activate a wand, a character must point it in the direction of the target or area, and make a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Smarts} or arcane skill roll (her choice) as an action. A failure uses a single charge. A @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{Critical Failure} also causes @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{Fatigue} to the wielder.

Wands are small and may be used in melee, while @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{grappling}, or other tight circumstances.

Charges: A wand has 25 charges when created, and cannot recharge. Each use consumes a charge.

Craft: The cost of a wand is equal to its Cost per Charge (its @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power’s} Rank × the number of @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Points} it uses multiplied by 50 gp) multipied by the number of charges. A fully-charged wand of dispel, for example, costs (100 × 25) 2,500 gp. A wand's market price is double its cost to craft
system.notes: 25 charges. Lower the specified Trait one die or two with a raise, opposed by Spirit -2 as a free action at the end of the target's turn.

system.description: Wizards, sorcerers, and other arcane types always want more power. This Edge grants them an additional 5 Power Points. Power Points may be selected more than once, but only once per Rank. It may be taken as often as desired at Legendary Rank, but each grants only 2 additional points.

Wizards, sorcerers, and other arcane types always want more power. This Edge grants them an additional 5 Power Points. Power Points may be selected more than once, but only once per Rank. It may be taken as often as desired at Legendary Rank, but each grants only 2 additional points.

Typically made of smooth hickory or yew.   

(@UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Smarts} or @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-skills.Spellcasting]{Spellcasting})

  • @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Bolt]{Bolt} with AP 2 (magic missile; 1 charge)
  • @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Blast]{Blast} LBT (fireball; 2 charges)
  • @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Barrier]{Barrier} (wall of force; 2 charge)
  • @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Bolt]{Bolt} with two additional targets (chain lightning; 5 charges)

Price: 82,000 gp;

Weight: 5 lbs.

Craft: All listed powers; 41,000 gp

Pearl OF power

Average in size and luster, this pearl allows the bearer to recast any one @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powersummari00]{power} they’ve already cast that day (including any @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{Power Modifiers}). The user must still make a skill roll to cast the power, but pays no Power Points.

The pearl only allows powers of the determined Rank or lower to be recast.

Price: 16,000 gp (Novice), 36,000 gp (Seasoned), or 64,000 (Veteran);

Weight: –.

Craft: Creator must be able to cast a spell of that level; 8,000 gp (Novice), 18,000 gp (Seasoned) or 32,000 (Veteran).

Resurrection

See power @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Resurrection]{here}.

Rank: Heroic

Power Points: 20

Range: Touch

Duration: Instant

School: Conjuration

Trappings: Elaborate ceremonies, magical amulets, divine intervention

Perhaps the greatest power of all is the ability to bring someone back from the dead. It can restore lost loves, bring solace to the grieving, and topple kingdoms.

Returning the dead to life requires a reasonably complete corpse no more than a year old. The caster then prays, meditates, chants, or otherwise concentrates on mending the body and pulling the spirit back to it for four hours.

Once done, the healer makes a casting roll at −4. If successful, the victim returns to life with three @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Wounds} and is Exhausted. With a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise}, she returns merely Exhausted.

Modifiers

See generic modifiers @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{here}

  • Greater Resurrection (+10): The boundaries of life and death no longer matter. A caster can restore the dead to life regardless of how long the target was dead and even without a corpse. To resurrect the dead in this fashion requires a twelve-hour ritual and leaves the caster Exhausted.
  • Power (+5): The hero can raise a spirit dead up to a decade for +5 @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Points}.
system.trapping: Elaborate ceremonies, magical amulets, divine intervention

system.description:
Time Stop

See power @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Time Stop]{here}.

Rank: Heroic

Power Points: 8

Range: Self

Duration: Instant

School: Transmutation

Trappings: A glowing hourglass, floating ice particles, blurring.

Time is a constant for most, but not this spellcaster. The spell slows time for all but the caster, granting her a new turn immediately after she finishes this one. With a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise}, she gets up to four actions on the turn instead of three and can ignore up to 2 points of @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Multi-Action} penalties. Time stop can only be cast once per round.
system.trapping: A glowing hourglass, floating ice particles, blurring.

system.description:
Scions of magical bloodlines, pawns of fate and destiny, or simply flukes of fickle magic, sorcerers look within themselves for arcane prowess. As they tap into powers few mortals can imagine, these magic-touched souls must learn to harness the power of their birthright.

While some seek to control their abilities, others give in to their magic, letting it rule their lives. Sorcerers live and breathe that which other spellcasters devote their lives to mastering. For them magic is more than a useful tool or a field of study; it is life itself.

  • Arcane Background (Sorcerer): Sorcerers use @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-skills.Spellcasting]{Spellcasting} as their arcane skill, but it is linked to Spirit instead of Smarts (if multiclassing as a Smarts-based spellcaster, use the better of the two Attributes). Light/Darkness

See power @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Light / Darkness]{here}.

Rank: Novice

Power Points: 2

Range: Smarts

Duration: 10 minutes

School: Evocation

Trappings: Illusionary torch, sunlight, darkness, thick fogs.

Light creates bright illumination in a Large Blast Template. With a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise}, the light can be focused into a 5″ (10 yard) beam as well.

Darkness blocks illumination in an area the size of a Large Blast Template, making the area Dark, or Pitch Dark with a raise (see @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{Illumination}).

If light and darkness overlap, they create a patch of Dim light (−2).

Modifiers

See generic modifiers @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{here}

  • Area Effect (+2): Light only. The light expands to the caster’s @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Smarts} in radius, with a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore02gear00.JournalEntryPage.02gear0000000000]{Range} of Smarts ×2. The light is sunlight and acts as natural sunlight.
  • True DARKNESS (+2): The darkness blocks Infravison, @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-abilities.Low Light Vision]{Low Light Vision}, and @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-abilities.Darkvision]{Darkvision}. It has no effect on magical sight such as @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Detect/Conceal Arcana]{detect arcana.}
  • Mobile (+1): The caster can move the template at a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{Pace} equal to his arcane skill die type as a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{limited free action} each round. He can also attach it to an inanimate object when first cast if desired. It cannot be cast on a living being.
system.trapping: Illusionary torch, sunlight, darkness, thick fogs

system.description:
Blind

See power @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Blind]{here}.

Rank: Novice

Power Points: 2

Range: Smarts

Duration: Instant

School: Necromancy

Trappings: Bright flash of light, sand in eyes, confusion.

Those affected by this malicious power suffer blurred vision or near-complete ­blindness with a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise}.

Success means the victim suffers a −2 penalty to all actions involving sight, or −4 with a raise.

The victim automatically tries to shake off the effect with a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Vigor} roll as a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{free action} at the end of his following turns. @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{Success} removes 2 points of penalties, and a raise removes the effect entirely.

Modifiers

See generic modifiers @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{here}

  • Strong (+1): The Vigor roll to shake off the effect is made at −2.

BAG OF HOLDING

This common-looking cloth sack is about 2’ by 4’ in size, but inside it opens into a vast nondimensional space. Whatever is put into the bag, it weighs a fixed amount. This weight, and the limits in weight and volume of the bag’s contents, depend on the bag’s type, as shown on the table below.

If overloaded, the bag of holding and all of its contents are destroyed and lost, forever. Likewise, if the bag is ruptured, it and all its contents are destroyed. The bag’s owner can sense the volume used, making it possible to manage the space without accidentally destroying it or its contents.

Storing or retrieving an item from the bag requires an action. Lifeforms put into the bag can live for 10 minutes before being @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Incapacitated}.

Bag Weight Max Weight Max Volume

Minor

25 lbs

500 lbs.

70 cubic ft.

Major

50 lbs

1,500 lbs.

250 cubic ft.

Price: 5,000 gp (minor), 10,000 (major); Weight: Special.

Craft: 2,500 gp (minor), 5,000 gp (major).

The first @Item[Revelation Quill]{revelation quills} were created by the followers of the Peacock Spirit. Since the fall of Thassilon, the method for creating them has been lost. The few revelation quills that remain today continue to function, drawing their revelations, it is said, from the Peacock Spirit.

The quill is fashioned from a peacock’s tail feather. Its nib is made of bone, and when held, the quill seems strangely heavy. If placed in an empty vial or other glass container of similar size and left there for an hour, a revelation quill fills that container with ink. The ink created is of a random color 50% of the time, otherwise the ink is black.

While a revelation quill can function as a standard writing implement, its true strength lies in its ability to answer questions. Once per day, if the user concentrates on a specific future goal, event, or activity occurring within the coming week, the revelation quill takes over and writes out a short response, often in the form of a cryptic rhyme or omen.

Once per week, the user may use the quill in the same way to contact another plane instead, asking up to 10 yes or no questions of the mysterious intellect that guides the revelation quill.

See power: @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Healing]{Healing}

Potions are alchemical concoctions enchanted with arcane energies. Drinking the potion (an action) activates the @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power} contained within immediately. Each potion can only contain one power, and each potion contains only one dose.

An individual can only benefit from one potion per round. If given more than one, roll randomly to see which one takes effect—all others are wasted.

A potion can be administered to an @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Incapacitated} or unconscious person (as an action).

Craft: The crafting cost of a minor potion is equal to its @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power’s} Rank × the number of @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Points} it uses; then multiply that total by 25 gp. A major potion doubles the final result (For example, a potion of minor healing without any modifiers is Novice (1) × 3 power point × 25 gp = 75 gp). A potion's market price is double their cost to craft
system.notes: Restores 2 Wounds less than an hour old.

system.description: Spell Synergy: When the theurge uses her Combined Spells ability she reduces the cost of each spell by one Power Point (to a minimum of 1).

Bracers OF armor

These wrist or arm guards surround the wearer with an invisible but tangible field of force. They grant an @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore02gear00.JournalEntryPage.02gear0000000000]{armor} bonus of +2 (minor) or +4 (major), which does not stack with worn armor (use the highest value). The bracers can be freely used regardless of Armor Interference or Armor Restriction.

Price: 16,000 gp (minor), 36,000 (major); Weight: 1.

Craft: @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Protection]{Protection}; 8,000 gp (minor), 18,000 (major).

Pearl OF power

Average in size and luster, this pearl allows the bearer to recast any one @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powersummari00]{power} they’ve already cast that day (including any @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{Power Modifiers}). The user must still make a skill roll to cast the power, but pays no Power Points.

The pearl only allows powers of the determined Rank or lower to be recast.

Price: 16,000 gp (Novice), 36,000 gp (Seasoned), or 64,000 (Veteran);

Weight: –.

Craft: Creator must be able to cast a spell of that level; 8,000 gp (Novice), 18,000 gp (Seasoned) or 32,000 (Veteran).

See power: @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Dispel]{Dispel}

Scrolls contain magical words, runes, and glyphs a user can activate by reading them aloud.

Each scroll has one @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power} inscribed onto it, including possible @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{Power Modifiers} if listed in its description. A character must have the scroll in hand and may not read more than one scroll per turn.

Reading a scroll is an action and requires a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Smarts} roll or arcane skill roll (caster’s choice). If the roll is successful, the power activates and the scroll fades. A reader may try again if the rolls fails, but a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{Critical Failure} means the scroll crumbles to dust without activating the power.

Craft: The cost of a scroll is equal to its @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power’s} Rank × the number of @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Points}Power Points} it uses; then multiply that total by 50 gp. A scroll of @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Bolt]{bolt} with AP 2, for example, is Novice (1) × 2 Power Points, × 50 gp = 100 gp. A scroll's market price is double its cost to craft
system.notes: Opposed roll of arcane skills. If used to counter, be on Hold and interrupt foe. No effect on permanent enchantments or natural abilities.

system.description: See power: @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Relief]{Relief}

Relief

See power @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-powers.Relief]{here}.

Rank: Novice

Power Points: 1

Range: Smarts

Duration: Instant/One Hour

School: Conjuration

Trappings: Prayer, glowing hands, sparkling spirals of magic, a cool breeze.

Relief allows the caster to recover from a negative condition or the effects of @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{Fatigue}.

Recover: The caster removes one of the following negative conditions: @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Shaken}, @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Distracted, or Vulnerable}. A @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise} on the arcane skill roll removes two conditions. If affecting more than one target (via the Additional Recipients modifier), the caster may choose which condition(s) to remove for each ally.

Relief can’t remove negative conditions caused by ongoing effects (@UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{Bound or Entangled}, @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Stunned}, etc.) unless the source of the effect is removed.

Numb: Reduces the recipient’s total penalties from @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Wounds} and Fatigue by one with success, or two with a raise. An Exhausted (−2) hero with three Wounds (−3), for example, reduces her total −5 penalty to −4 with success, and −3 with a raise.

This effect lasts one hour. It doesn’t actually remove the Wounds or the Fatigue, it just allows the recipient to ignore the penalties. If the target would be @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Incapacitated} by Wounds or Fatigue, she’s still Incapacitated as usual.

Modifiers

See generic modifiers @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{here}

  • Additional Recipients (+1): The power may affect more than one target for 1 additional @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Point} each.
  • Restoration (+3): The caster can restore one die type from an energy draining effect with a success, two with a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{raise}. See @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-abilities.Energy Drain]{Energy Drain}.
  • Stunned (+1): If using the Recover option, the caster may also affect the Stunned condition. This completely removes the effects of being @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03combat00000000]{Stunned}.
system.trapping: Prayer, Prayer, glowing hands, sparkling spirals of magic, a cool breeze.

A wand is a thin baton that casts a single @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power}, including any @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powermodifie00]{Power Modifiers}, each time it's activated. To activate a wand, a character must point it in the direction of the target or area, and make a @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore01charac.JournalEntryPage.01traitsummari00]{Smarts} or arcane skill roll (her choice) as an action. A failure uses a single charge. A @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03rules000000000]{Critical Failure} also causes @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{Fatigue} to the wielder.

Wands are small and may be used in melee, while @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore03rules0.JournalEntryPage.03situationalr00]{grappling}, or other tight circumstances.

Charges: A wand has 25 charges when created, and cannot recharge. Each use consumes a charge.

Craft: The cost of a wand is equal to its Cost per Charge (its @UUID[Compendium.swpf-core-rules.swpf-rules.swpfcore05powers.JournalEntryPage.05powers00000001]{power’s} Rank × the number of @Compendium[swpf-core-rules.swpf-edges.Power Points]{Power Points} it uses multiplied by 50 gp) multipied by the number of charges. A fully-charged wand of dispel, for example, costs (100 × 25) 2,500 gp. A wand's market price is double its cost to craft
system.notes: 25 charges. Raise the specified Trait one die or two with a raise.
